title: Type I and Type II Errors, Power

epigraph.text: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity than one who is perverse in speech and is a fool.
epigraph.quote: Every test of significance involves a choice between two kinds of error.

content.text:
Every hypothesis test risks two kinds of errors. Understanding them 
is essential for responsible statistical practice.

content.title: Type I and Type II Errors
content.content:
- **Type I Error**: Rejecting $H_0$ when 
 it is true (false positive). $P(\text{Type I}) = \alpha$.

- **Type II Error**: Failing to reject 
 $H_0$ when it is false (false negative). $P(\text{Type II}) = \beta$.

content.title: Power
content.content:
The **power** of a test is the probability of 
correctly rejecting $H_0$ when it is false:
\[
 \text{Power} = 1 - \beta = P(\text{Reject } H_0 \mid H_0 \text{ is false})
\]

items: Larger significance level $\alpha$ (but this increases Type I error)

content.text:
We cannot minimize both errors simultaneously. The choice depends on 
consequences:

items: Medical trials: Type I error (approving harmful drug) may be worse
Screening tests: Type II error (missing disease) may be worse

marginNotes.text:
He who justifies the wicked and he 
who condemns the righteous are both alike an abomination to the Lord.
